trade.summary: Trade relations significantly impacted by Western sanctions on Russia following Ukraine invasion. Mexico has not formally joined sanctions but has reduced engagement. Residual trade includes fertilizers, steel, and some chemical products from Russia.

diplomatic.summary: Mexico has maintained a degree of neutrality on the Ukraine war, abstaining on some UN votes, but has not formally condemned Russia nor joined sanctions. Relations have cooled due to Russian disinformation campaigns targeting Latin America and Mexico's growing alignment with Western positions on rule-of-law.
recent_incidents: Mexico abstained on multiple UN resolutions condemning Russian invasion of Ukraine

military.summary: No significant military relationship. Historical Soviet-era political sympathy has faded. Mexico has no military cooperation agreements with Russia.

financial.summary: Minimal financial ties. Russian entities largely excluded from Mexican financial markets due to secondary sanctions risk. Limited direct investment in either direction.

energy.summary: Both are significant oil producers but direct bilateral energy trade is negligible. Mexico imports some Russian fertilizers needed for agriculture.

scientific.summary: Scientific cooperation has diminished since 2022. Some legacy academic exchange programs remain but are largely inactive.
